sourceMovie.title: Gary
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Gary (2026) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kodachrome
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gary worked for you, Kodachrome (2017) is a strong next pick. The two films share drama and comedy throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

recommendations.title: Code 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gary worked for you, Code 3 (2025) is a strong next pick. The two films share drama and comedy throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

recommendations.title: ¿Y cómo es él?
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Gary and ¿Y cómo es él? (2022) are not the same movie, but they rhyme in the ways that matter. Each commits to drama and comedy, brings comic timing and sharper banter, and trusts the audience to stay with a bold swing.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gary?
faq.answer: Try Kodachrome, Code 3, ¿Y cómo es él?—each shares DNA with Gary while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gary?
faq.answer: Gary (2026) is best described as Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gary worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
